author: There
text:
Decompose five files riding the 300-line cap into sibling modules

Five source/test files sat at 293-299 lines, one line short of failing the >=300 pre-commit gate. Each splits a cohesive unit into a sibling module that the original re-exports, per the project decomposition convention, so callers stay byte-stable. Pure code movement — coverage holds at 100% (6241/6241).

store.rs -> store_archive.rs: the state-branch write methods (commit_task, close_and_archive, all_tasks) move to a second impl Store block; they use only public accessors, so no field visibility widened. state_repo.rs -> state_repo_symlinks.rs: the .balls/ symlink materializers, pub(crate)-re-exported. participant.rs -> participant_projection.rs: Field + Projection, pub-re-exported, with participant_projection_tests reattached there. ready_deps.rs -> links.rs: the typed-link tests (and the closed_task helper) that had accreted onto the ready/dep file. plugin_participant_enforce.rs -> plugin_participant_enforce_close.rs: the bl-close and SPEC-5.1 context tests; the shared jira_policy fixture is hoisted into common::native_plugin.

cli_command.rs (299) is left as-is and noted: it is a single clap Subcommand enum and cannot be split without restructuring the CLI surface. Gate verified green: line-length, cargo build --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -D warnings, 1217 tests, 100% coverage.
